description_long: This object is a vintage rotary dial corded telephone, featuring a robust metal and Bakelite construction with ornate metallic detailing. The base is heavy and intricately designed, with a large rotary dial on the front and a hand-crank on the side, suggesting it is an early 20th-century model. The handset is connected to the base by a coiled cord and rests on a decorative cradle with brass fixtures. The earpiece and mouthpiece are both cylindrical and attached to the handset, which is supported by two ornate hooks. The overall color scheme is a combination of black and brass, giving it a classic, antique appearance. This type of phone would typically be found in historical urban settings, such as old offices or homes.
description: A vintage rotary dial corded telephone with ornate brass and black detailing, a coiled cord, and a hand-crank on the side. The handset rests on a decorative cradle above a heavy, intricately designed base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rotary dial, ornate base, and the handset resting on the cradle.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the hand-crank and the profile of the handset and cradle.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the base, the cradle, and the coiled cord.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other side of the base and the coiled cord hanging down.
